arts.ttl: RB Leipzig’s interest in Jan-Niklas Beste cools as they focus on a backup for Benjamin Henrichs
arts.sum: The Leipziger Volkszeitung reports that RB Leipzig’s interest in Benfica midfielder Jan-Niklas Beste has now cooled as they look to address other areas of their squad first. Leipzig were interested…

arts.ttl: Torino target Benfica’s Cabral in striker search
arts.sum: Torino have intensified their pursuit of a new striker, with Benfica's Arthur Cabral emerging as the latest target. The Italian club has submitted an initial offer to bring the Brazilian forward back to Serie A, where he previously played for Fiorentina. The post Torino target Benfica’s Cabral in striker search appeared first on FootItalia.com.

arts.ttl: Matheus Cunha had ban reduced for offering to buy security guard new glasses
arts.sum: Player involved in melee after Wolves’ loss to Ipswich Brazilian’s offer was considered as a mitigating factor Matheus Cunha’s offer to buy new glasses for an Ipswich security guard with whom he was involved in an altercation helped convince an independent disciplinary panel to cut his ban from three matches to two. The Wolves forward was charged by the Football Association on 16 December after he was seen confronting members of Ipswich’s security team after his side’s last-gasp loss at Molineux two days earlier. That included him grabbing the glasses of a security guard in the melee. Continue reading...

arts.ttl: Wolves star Matheus Cunha has FA ban reduced after two gestures to Ipswich staff member
arts.sum: Forward Matheus Cunha was handed a two-match ban after he was involved in a post-match brawl following Wolverhampton Wanderer's defeat by Ipswich Town in the Premier League

arts.ttl: Wolves star Matheus Cunha: Two reasons why Brazilian only received two-match FA ban for Ipswich Town incident
arts.sum: The Football Association reveals why Wolverhampton Wanderers forward Matheus Cunha only received a two-match ban for an incident involving a member of Ipswich Town's security staff.

arts.ttl: David Ornstein shares whether Matheus Cunha will have a release clause in his new Wolves contract
